classes.label: Apple Scab Leaf
classes.symptoms: Dark, scabby or corky lesions on leaves and fruit; velvety olive spots on undersides in humid weather.
classes.management: Rake fallen leaves, prune for airflow, apply sulfur or approved fungicides per label timing; choose resistant cultivars where available.

classes.label: Apple leaf
classes.symptoms: Typical apple foliage without scab or rust pustules; used as healthy baseline in the dataset.
classes.management: Balanced fertility, avoid leaf wetness overnight, scout early season for primary scab/rust.

classes.label: Apple rust leaf
classes.symptoms: Orange-yellow pustules on leaf underside; may cause premature leaf drop near junipers (alternate host).
classes.management: Remove nearby alternate hosts where feasible; fungicide programs during spring leaf emergence.

classes.label: Bell_pepper leaf
classes.symptoms: Healthy pepper foliage appearance in field images.
classes.management: Avoid overhead irrigation; manage aphids to reduce virus pressure.

classes.label: Bell_pepper leaf spot
classes.symptoms: Circular or angular spots with yellow halos; may coalesce under wet conditions.
classes.management: Improve ventilation, avoid working wet plants, copper or approved bactericides when bacterial; rotate crops.

classes.label: Blueberry leaf
classes.symptoms: Typical healthy blueberry leaves in natural scenes.
classes.management: Acidic soil (pH ~4.5–5.5), consistent moisture, mulch to stabilize roots.

classes.label: Cherry leaf
classes.symptoms: Healthy cherry foliage; dataset includes sour/cherry types in field context.
classes.management: Sanitation for leaf spot pathogens, dormant fungicides where historically needed.

classes.label: Corn Gray leaf spot
classes.symptoms: Rectangular gray-tan lesions between leaf veins; common in humid corn belts.
classes.management: Resistant hybrids, crop rotation, tillage to reduce residue; fungicides if threshold exceeded.

classes.label: Corn leaf blight
classes.symptoms: Elongated tan lesions; northern/southern blight complexes vary by pattern and spore types.
classes.management: Resistant varieties, residue management, timely fungicide in high-value seed fields.

classes.label: Corn rust leaf
classes.symptoms: Rust pustules (orange to cinnamon) on upper/lower leaf surfaces.
classes.management: Usually late-season; monitor if defoliation threatens yield; fungicide if economic threshold met.

classes.label: grape leaf
classes.symptoms: Healthy grape foliage baseline in the dataset.
classes.management: Canopy management for sun and airflow; integrated mildew/black rot programs regionally.

classes.label: grape leaf black rot
classes.symptoms: Tan ‘shot-hole’ lesions, black fruiting bodies; fruit may shrivel (mummies).
classes.management: Sanitation (mummies), canopy openness, protectant fungicides at bloom/fruit set.

classes.label: Peach leaf
classes.symptoms: Healthy peach leaves; contrast with curl or shot-hole diseases in other datasets.
classes.management: Dormant copper for curl where applicable; avoid excessive nitrogen.

classes.label: Potato leaf
classes.symptoms: Healthy potato foliage reference.
classes.management: Hilling, blight scouting, certified seed, avoid overhead irrigation late day.

classes.label: Potato leaf early blight
classes.symptoms: Concentric ring spots (‘target’), often lower canopy first; Alternaria complex.
classes.management: Nutrition balance, reduce stress, fungicide programs in humid seasons.

classes.label: Potato leaf late blight
classes.symptoms: Water-soaked patches turning brown; white sporulation under humid nights.
classes.management: Resistant varieties where possible, eliminate culls, fungicide during conducive weather.

classes.label: Raspberry leaf
classes.symptoms: Healthy raspberry leaf appearance.
classes.management: Prune for airflow; virus vectors (aphids) management in cane fruit.

classes.label: Soyabean leaf
classes.symptoms: Healthy soybean foliage reference.
classes.management: Crop rotation, seed treatments; scout for rust/frogeye regionally.

classes.label: Squash Powdery mildew leaf
classes.symptoms: White powdery patches on upper leaf surfaces; spreads quickly in warm dry days + humid nights.
classes.management: Resistant cultivars, sulfur/biologicals; remove heavily infected leaves early.

classes.label: Strawberry leaf
classes.symptoms: Healthy strawberry foliage baseline.
classes.management: Mulch, avoid leaf wetness, rotate beds; manage mites and angular leaf spot regionally.

classes.label: Tomato Early blight leaf
classes.symptoms: Target-like lesions, often on older leaves first; stem collar lesions possible.
classes.management: Mulch, staking, fungicide on schedule in wet seasons; remove lowest infected leaves cautiously.

classes.label: Tomato leaf
classes.symptoms: Healthy tomato foliage reference.
classes.management: Even watering, calcium for blossom end rot prevention, good ventilation in tunnels.

classes.label: Tomato leaf bacterial spot
classes.symptoms: Small dark specks with yellow halos; may look ‘shot’ on fruit too.
classes.management: Use certified seed/transplants, avoid overhead water, copper programs early; rotate fields.

classes.label: Tomato leaf late blight
classes.symptoms: Greasy irregular lesions; white sporulation on leaf margins in humidity.
classes.management: Resistant varieties, rapid removal of infected plants in gardens; fungicide in outbreaks.

classes.label: Tomato leaf mosaic virus
classes.symptoms: Mottling, fern-like distortion, stunting depending on virus complex.
classes.management: Remove infected plants; control aphids; sanitize tools; virus-free seed.

classes.label: Tomato leaf yellow virus
classes.symptoms: Yellowing, curling, stunting—often vectored by whiteflies in tropics/subtropics.
classes.management: Reflective mulch, whitefly management, resistant varieties where available.

classes.label: Tomato mold leaf
classes.symptoms: Fuzzy mold on senescing tissue or high-humidity canopies; secondary invaders possible.
classes.management: Dehumidify greenhouse air, leaf removal for airflow, targeted fungicides.

classes.label: Tomato Septoria leaf spot
classes.symptoms: Small circular spots with dark borders and tan centers, tiny black pycnidia specks.
classes.management: Mulch soil to reduce splash, remove infected debris, fungicide rotation in wet years.

classes.label: Tomato two spotted spider mites leaf
classes.symptoms: Fine stippling, bronzing, webbing in heavy infestations.
classes.management: Avoid dusty conditions, predatory mites, horticultural oils/soaps per crop stage.

classes.label: Coffee Health Leaves
classes.symptoms: Dark green, glossy, vibrant leaves; no spotting or yellowing.
classes.management: Maintain regular watering and partial shade.

classes.label: Coffee Leaf Rust
classes.symptoms: Yellow to orange powdery spots on leaf undersides; pale spots on top.
classes.management: Prune for airflow, use rust-resistant cultivars, apply copper fungicides.

classes.label: Coffee Phoma
classes.symptoms: Dark irregular necrotic spots on leaves, often on margins with rings.
classes.management: Windbreaks, proper drainage, remove infected branches.
